info.name: CareConnect Nurse — Backend API (v2 — mobile-contract bodies)
info.description:
## CareConnect Nurse Backend API — v2

**What changed in v2:** form bodies now match the mobile contract from
BACKEND_API.md (camelCase, exact field names per §9). The architecture is unchanged
(still a single generic endpoint).

### Quick start
1. Set the `baseUrl` collection variable (default: `https://staging.goconnect.com/api`).
2. Run `Login` (under Auth & Profile). Copy the `accessToken` from the response into
   the `accessToken` collection variable.
3. All other authenticated requests use `{{accessToken}}` automatically.

### Response envelope
- **Success:** `{ "data": <payload> }` (or 204 No Content)
- **Error:** `{ "message": "..." }` (with optional `errors` for 422)

### Visit forms — generic endpoint
All 22 visit-form endpoints from BACKEND_API.md are collapsed into ONE:
```
POST /api/visits/{id}/forms/{form_name}
```
The body is whatever the mobile sends. The backend stores it as-is in `patient_meta.value`.

**The 12 whitelisted forms:**
- **Class A (Merge):** `flowsheet`, `allergies`, `social-assessment`
- **Class B (Append):** `nursing-progress-note`, `progress-notes`, `refusal`, `referral`,
  `sari_screening`, `inventory_usage`, `incidents`, `blood-sugar`, `visual-triage-checklist`

**Flow sheet** is special: 15 sections, one endpoint, mobile sends one section at a time.
See the `flowsheet — 15 sections` sub-folder.

**Progress notes** is special: doctor + social_worker share the same form_name with a
`type` discriminator in the body.

### Headers
- `Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}`
- `Accept: application/json`
- `Content-Type: application/json` (for JSON bodies)
- `Accept-Language: {{lang}}` (`en` or `ar`)

request.description:
Sign in with username + password. The app stores `accessToken` in `AsyncStorage`.

**Note:** the response shape is `{ data: { accessToken, user } }`.
The `user` object has the same shape as `GET /me`.

response.name: 200 Success

request.description: Start the registration flow. Sends an OTP. Continue with /auth/verify-otp.

response.name: 422 Validation Failed

request.description:
Verify the OTP sent during registration or password reset.

- `purpose`: either `register` or `reset_password`
- When `purpose=reset_password`, the response contains a `resetToken` to be used in /auth/reset-password.
Sends a reset OTP to the user's email.
Use the `resetToken` returned by /auth/verify-otp (purpose=reset_password) to set a new password.
Change the authenticated user's password. Requires `change_password` permission.

item.description:
**Flow Sheet — 15 sections, one endpoint.**

All flow-sheet sections share the same endpoint:
```
POST /api/visits/{id}/forms/flowsheet
```

The mobile app saves **one section at a time**. Each request has a single top-level key
in the body — that key identifies which section is being saved.

**Class A — Merge:** the backend stores a single `patient_meta` row per (visit_id, form='flowsheet').
Subsequent saves of OTHER sections shallow-merge into the same row's `value` JSON, preserving
previously saved sections.

**Permission per section:** the middleware inspects the section key in the body (not the URL)
to pick the matching permission rule. See each section's request for the specific rule.

**The 15 sections:**
- 9.1.1 `outside_dialysis` — flag for off-site session
- 9.1.2 `pre_treatment_vitals` — vitals + BP site/method
- 9.1.3 `machines` — dialysis machine model
- 9.1.4 `pain_assessment` — pain score + characterisation
- 9.1.5 `fall_risk` — Morse fall scale
- 9.1.6 `nursing_actions` — clinical actions log (replaces array)
- 9.1.7 `dialysis_parameters` — time-series machine readings (replaces array)
- 9.1.8 `alarms_test` — alarms tested OK flag
- 9.1.9 `intake_output` — fluids in/out
- 9.1.10 `car` — dialyzer/circuit info
- 9.1.11 `access` — vascular access description
- 9.1.12 `dialysate` — dialysate composition (Na, HCO3, K, glucose)
- 9.1.13 `anticoagulation` — anticoagulant type
- 9.1.14 `medications` — per-medication admin record
- 9.1.15 `post_treatment` — **multipart** with 2 signatures

request.description:
Append a new SARI (Severe Acute Respiratory Infection) screening to visit 5.

**Permission:** `submit_sari_screening`
**Storage:** Class B (Append) — new row each save.

**Request body** (per BACKEND_API.md §9.7):
- `addressographPatientName` — string (often equals the visit's patientName)
- `dateTime` — ISO 8601, when the screening was performed
- `sariFeatures` — 3-state values (`"yes"`, `"no"`, or `null`):
  - `fever`, `coughOrBreathing`, `radiographicEvidence`
- `exposureCriteria` — 3-state values (`"yes"`, `"no"`, or `null`):
  - `closeContactSari`, `travelToPhacNotice`,
    `recentExposurePotentialSource`, `inconsistentWithOtherKnownCause`
- `actions` — 3-state values (`"done"`, `"not_done"`, or `null`):
  - `thinkInfectionControl`, `tellMedicalHealthOfficer`,
    `tellInfectionControl`, `consultInfectiousDiseaseSpecialist`, `test`

request.description: Demonstrates the whitelist rejection. Any form_name not in the allowed list returns 422.

item.description:
**Generic visit-form endpoint** with mobile-contract bodies.

URL pattern: `POST /api/visits/{id}/forms/{form_name}`

Architecture stays generic — one endpoint, form_name in the URL, JSON body whatever the
mobile sends. The backend stores the JSON as-is in `patient_meta.value`.

**Body schemas match BACKEND_API.md §9 exactly** (camelCase, exact field names).

**Two storage strategies:**
- **Class A (Merge):** `flowsheet`, `allergies`, `social-assessment` — one row per
  (visit_id, form), updated in place via shallow merge.
- **Class B (Append):** all other forms — a new row is created on every save.

**Special case — `flowsheet`:** the mobile saves ONE section at a time. Each request has a
single top-level section key (`pre_treatment_vitals`, `pain_assessment`, etc.). The Class A
merge accumulates them into a single row. See the flowsheet sub-folder for all 15 sections.

**Special case — `progress-notes`:** the body has a `type` discriminator (`"doctor"` or
`"social_worker"`) so one endpoint handles both author roles. The middleware picks the
permission based on `type` (not the URL).

**All file uploads** (signatures, attachments) go through `MediaService` and are tracked by
`*_media_id` keys inside the JSON value. On read, the response includes parallel `*_url` keys.

**Whitelist:** an unknown form_name returns 422. The 12 whitelisted forms are documented in
each sub-request.
